corpus_source_description: The TASK-011 benchmark corpus is the set of on-disk JSON artifacts under benchmarks/cases/. There is no Python-side case generator. approved_cases() is a read-only loader that reads each task011_case_*.json file from disk.
case_path_pattern_description: Each case is one independently reviewable JSON file named task011_case_NN.json where NN is the zero-padded decimal index in [01, 20]. The validator reads these files by case_id and recomputes their canonical hashes from on-disk contents.
manifest_path_description: The manifest binds case_hashes to recomputed canonical hashes from the artifact set. case_ids must point to existing files; missing artifacts or hash mismatches are rejection-level errors per contract section 19.1.
validation_entrypoint_description: Exit 0 means the corpus passes every section 19.1 validation form plus the section 9.3 transition table execution.
loader_module_description: Read-only artifact loader. approved_cases() reads benchmarks/cases/task011_case_*.json; it does NOT synthesize case payloads. case_file_path(root, case_id) maps case_id -> file path.

approval_authority.approval_scope: TASK-011 benchmark corpus approval (all 20 case artifacts + manifest)

synthetic_case_ids_description: These case IDs MUST be listed in manifest.synthetic_case_ids and MUST NOT be cited as independent validation evidence (contract section 9.2.5).
